def CreateProjectRecord(name: str, description: str, owner_id: int) -> int:
    from datetime import datetime
    project = {
        'name': name,
        'description': description,
        'owner_id': owner_id,
        'status': 'planning',
        'created_at': datetime.now()
    }
    result = create_project(project)
    return result['project_id']

def UpdateProject(project_data: dict) -> dict:
    # Step 1: Check if project exists
    check_result = CheckProjectExists(project_data)
    if not check_result.get('success', True):
        return check_result
    project = check_result
    
    # Step 2: Update project fields
    updated_project = UpdateProjectFields(project_data, project)
    
    # Step 3: Record status change if status changed
    status_changed = RecordStatusChange(project, updated_project)
    
    # Step 4: Return success result
    return {
        'success': True,
        'message': 'Project updated successfully',
        'data': updated_project
    }

UpdateTaskStatus.behavior: Check task exists via tasks.get. Validate status transition: allowed transitions: todo->in_progress, in_progress->review, review->done, any->blocked, blocked->previous. If invalid, return error. If new_status is 'done', record actual_hours from input. Update task via tasks.update. Return success.
postconditions: Task status updated, actual_hours recorded if done

CompleteTask.purpose: Complete a task from review to done. Record actual_hours. Release member (set available).
CompleteTask.behavior: Check task exists via tasks.get. If status is not 'review', return error. Update task status to 'done' and record actual_hours via tasks.update. If task has assignee_id, update member status to 'available' via members.update. Return success.
postconditions: Task status set to done, actual_hours recorded, member set to available if assigned

data_operations.description: Read and update task to done

DeleteTask.purpose: Delete a task. Check task exists. If assigned, release member.
DeleteTask.behavior: Check task exists via tasks.get. If not, return error. If task has assignee_id, update member status to 'available' via members.update. Delete task via tasks.delete. Return success.
postconditions: Task deleted, member released if assigned

decomposition_rationale: The parent Project_prd handles multiple commands. Each command is decomposed into a separate child function that directly implements that command. The parent will parse the command and call the appropriate child. Children cover all functional requirements: CreateProject (FR-001), UpdateProject (FR-002), DeleteProject (FR-003), CreateTask (FR-004), AssignTask (FR-005), UpdateTaskStatus (FR-006), CompleteTask (FR-007), DeleteTask (FR-008). Remaining commands (list_project_tasks, get_member_tasks, get_project_progress, add_member, update_member_availability) are not decomposed here due to max children limit; they would be additional children if needed. Each child directly accesses data stores via requested_capabilities and declares global_vars subset.

subprd.purpose: Project Task Manager
subprd.description:
INPUT FORMAT:
  Format: json
  Description: Input is a JSON object with command and project_data fields. The command specifies the operation, and project_data contains the parameters.
  Schema:
    command: string (one of: create_project, update_project, delete_project, create_task, assign_task, update_task_status, complete_task, delete_task, list_project_tasks, get_member_tasks, get_project_progress, add_member, update_member_availability)
    project_data: object with fields depending on command
  Examples:
    {"command": "create_project", "project_data": {"name": "\u7f51\u7ad9\u91cd\u6784", "description": "\u91cd\u6784\u516c\u53f8\u5b98\u7f51", "owner_id": 6}}

OUTPUT FORMAT:
  Format: json
  Description: Output is a JSON object with success, message, and data fields.
  Schema:
    success: boolean
    message: string
    data: object (varies by command)
  Examples:
    {"success": true, "message": "\u9879\u76ee\u521b\u5efa\u6210\u529f", "data": {"project_id": 1}}

Functional Requirements:
[FR-001] Create Project: Create a new project with name, description, owner_id. Check owner exists. Default status planning. Record creation time.
[FR-002] Update Project: Update project info: description, owner_id, status, deadline. Check project exists. Record status changes.
[FR-003] Delete Project: Delete project and all its tasks. Check project exists.
[FR-004] Create Task: Create a task under a project with title, description, priority, estimated_hours. Check project exists and status is planning or active. Default status todo. No assignee.
[FR-005] Assign Task: Assign task to a member. Check task exists and unassigned or reassign. Check member exists and available. Optionally check skills. Update assignee_id and member status to busy.
[FR-006] Update Task Status: Update task status following flow: todo->in_progress->review->done. Blocked can be set anytime. Record actual_hours when done.
[FR-007] Complete Task: Complete a task from review to done. Record actual_hours. Release member (set available).
[FR-008] Delete Task: Delete a task. Check task exists. If assigned, release member.
[FR-009] List Project Tasks: List tasks of a project with optional filters: status, priority, assignee. Show task details and progress.
[FR-010] Get Member Tasks: Get all tasks of a member: current and historical. Calculate total hours.
[FR-011] Get Project Progress: Get project progress: completion rate, task counts by status, estimated vs actual hours, estimated completion time.
[FR-012] Add Member: Add a new team member. Check member_id uniqueness.
[FR-013] Update Member Availability: Update member availability. Can only set to offline if no in-progress tasks.

constraints.description: Storage: memory - Three independent data sources: members, projects, tasks

acceptance_criteria.description: Create project with valid owner returns success with project_id and status planning.
Create project with invalid owner returns error.
Update project status to active sets deadline correctly.
Delete project removes project and all its tasks.
Create task under active project returns task with status todo.
Assign task to available member updates assignee and member status to busy.
Assign task to busy member returns error.
Update task status from todo to in_progress succeeds.
Complete task from review sets status done and releases member.
Delete task releases member if assigned.
List project tasks with status filter returns correct tasks.
Get member tasks returns current and completed tasks with total hours.
Get project progress returns correct completion rate and hours.
Add member with unique ID succeeds.
Update member availability to offline only if no in-progress tasks.

fix_summary.suggestion: If repair_action is redecompose, add/rewrite child boundaries so every child input has a source and parent does not access resources directly.

CreateProject.purpose: Create a new project with name, description, owner_id. Check owner exists. Default status planning. Record creation time.

inputs.name: project_data
inputs.description: Project data with name, description, owner_id

outputs.name: result
outputs.description: Result with success, message, data containing project_id

CreateProject.behavior: Check owner exists via members.get. If not, return error. Create project via projects.create with status 'planning' and current timestamp. Return success with project_id.
postconditions: Project created with status planning and creation_time

UpdateProject.purpose: Update project info: description, owner_id, status, deadline. Check project exists. Record status changes.

inputs.name: project_data
inputs.description: Project data with project_id and fields to update

UpdateProject.behavior: Check project exists via projects.get. If not, return error. Update project fields via projects.update. If status changed, record change (e.g., in a log or update timestamp). Return success.
postconditions: Project updated with provided fields, status change recorded if status updated

DeleteProject.purpose: Delete project and all its tasks. Check project exists.
DeleteProject.behavior: Check project exists via projects.get. If not, return error. List all tasks of project via tasks.list with filter project_id. Delete each task via tasks.delete. Delete project via projects.delete. Return success.
postconditions: Project and all its tasks deleted

CreateTask.purpose: Create a task under a project with title, description, priority, estimated_hours. Check project exists and status is planning or active. Default status todo. No assignee.

inputs.name: project_data
inputs.description: Project data with project_id and task fields

outputs.name: result
outputs.description: Result with success, message, data containing task_id

CreateTask.behavior: Check project exists via projects.get. If not, return error. Check project status is planning or active; if not, return error. Create task via tasks.create with status 'todo' and no assignee. Return success with task_id.
preconditions: project_data contains project_id, title, description, priority, estimated_hours
postconditions: Task created with status todo and no assignee

AssignTask.purpose: Assign task to a member. Check task exists and unassigned or reassign. Check member exists and available. Optionally check skills. Update assignee_id and member status to busy.

inputs.name: project_data
inputs.description: Project data with task_id, member_id, optional skill check

AssignTask.behavior: Check task exists via tasks.get. If not, return error. Check task is unassigned or allow reassign. Check member exists via members.get and is available (status != 'busy'). If not, return error. Optionally check skills. Update task assignee_id via tasks.update. Update member status to 'busy' via members.update. Return success.
postconditions: Task assignee updated, member status set to busy
